title: Our Rosehill College Way
metaDescription: Our Rosehill College Way — the values of Manaakitanga, Rangatiratanga, Whanaungatanga and Hihiritanga, plus uniform and attendance expectations.

sections.heading: Our Rosehill College Way
sections.lede: Manaakitanga, Rangatiratanga, Whanaungatanga and Hihiritanga — four values that shape how we connect, reflect, respect and strive every day, alongside clear expectations for attendance and uniform.

image.alt: Rosehill College students living the RHC Way

sections.heading: What do we expect from you?
sections.html:
We expect students to attend school every day, as consistent attendance is crucial for developing lifelong learning skills and achieving higher educational outcomes, which contribute to better health, higher incomes, job stability, and stronger, more connected communities.

At Rosehill College, we also expect students to be in their uniform as it fosters a sense of belonging and pride within our school community. Wearing a uniform promotes equality, reduces distractions, and helps create a focused learning environment. It reflects our commitment to maintaining high standards and discipline, ensuring that all students present themselves in a respectful and unified manner. Uniforms also prepare students for future professional settings where dress codes are common, instilling a sense of responsibility and readiness for the world beyond school.

image.alt: Rosehill College students in uniform at school

sections.heading: Seniors uniform
sections.html: The expectation for students to wear their uniforms at Rosehill College is grounded in the belief that it promotes unity and school spirit. Uniforms eliminate socio-economic disparities, allowing students to focus on their education rather than fashion.

sections.heading: Juniors uniform
sections.html: Wearing our uniform supports a disciplined atmosphere and enhances the school's identity, making students easily recognisable as members of our institution. It also encourages students to take pride in their appearance and fosters a professional mindset that benefits them in future academic and career endeavours.

sections.heading: Every day counts
sections.lede: One or two days a week does not seem that much, but the missed learning adds up — and children learn best at the start of the day, so arriving late to school or class matters too.

images.alt: Chart showing how missing one or two days a week adds up over a school year
images.caption: 1 or 2 days a week does not seem that much but...

images.alt: Chart showing the impact of missed school days on learning
Chart showing the impact of arriving late to school and class
images.caption: Late for school, late to class? Children learn best at the start of the day.

sections.heading: Attendance Management Plan
sections.lede: Read our full plan for supporting every student to attend school every day.

sections.heading: Connect

image.alt: Rosehill College students connecting with each other — Manaakitanga

sections.html:
"We rise by lifting others"

  • We use mana enhancing, respectful, inclusive words and actions
  • We look after our learning environment
  • We are organised and prepared to learn
  • We bring the equipment we need, including charged laptops/Chromebooks

Because:

  • Connecting and learning collectively helps everyone
  • Being prepared for our learning keeps us focused on achieving
  • Everyone has the right to a safe and enjoyable learning environment

sections.heading: Reflect

image.alt: Rosehill College students celebrating success together — Rangatiratanga

sections.html:
"Excellence in all that we do"

  • We celebrate success together
  • Knowing what you need to learn to achieve excellence
  • Reflect on my learning and my actions every day to strive to be better

Because:

  • We care about learning together
  • My learning is my responsibility and I want to be better
  • We care about self leadership
  • Achieving excellence is everyone's right

sections.heading: Respect

image.alt: Rosehill College students showing respect for each other — Whanaungatanga

sections.html:
"Together we are more"

  • We arrive on time
  • We wear our uniform correctly
  • We arrive with headphones/phones away
  • We respect others' ideas and contributions — everyone is unique

Because:

  • We care about our school community whānau
  • We are ready to greet each other
  • We know phones are not learning devices
  • We care about learning together
  • Our uniform represents commitment to our school community and our readiness to learn

sections.heading: Strive

image.alt: Rosehill College students striving for excellence — Hihiritanga

sections.html:
"We are resilient and persevere"

  • We support each other through challenges
  • We follow the 'RHC Way' and encourage others to do the same
  • We know mistakes are moments of learning
  • We are determined to achieve excellence

Because:

  • We encourage personal and collective excellence
  • We care about serving others
  • We are active listeners and contributors

sections.heading: Live the Rosehill College Way
sections.text: Our values come to life in classrooms, on the field and across our community. Start your enrolment, or explore our vision and values.
